body: How do I check the status of my overtime grievance? It's been a couple weeks.
Your grievance is at Step 2 — the hearing is set for next Tuesday and I'm representing you.
Good to hear. I'll be there. Thanks for staying on it.
My dues line came out higher than the posted rate this month — is that right?
Yes — the dues adjustment the membership voted in December took effect this pay period. Nothing wrong on your stub.
Something happened on my line I think breaks the contract. Where do I file a grievance?
Grab the intake form at the hall front desk or on local247aaw.org, fill in the date and what happened, and a steward will help with the rest. You've got 14 days.
Got it, thank you.
The body shop heat is brutal again and the spot coolers aren't keeping up. Can the safety committee look?
I've scheduled temperature readings during peak heat Thursday. If we exceed the contract threshold we can require relief breaks and added cooling.
We just had a baby — how do I add him to my health coverage and what's the deadline?
You have 30 days from the birth date. Fill out the dependent enrollment form at the benefits office and bring the birth certificate when it's ready.
I've got a disciplinary meeting Friday over an attendance write-up. Can a steward sit in with me?
Yes — that's your right to representation. I'll be there. Bring any doctor's notes and don't answer questions until I'm in the room.
Thank you — that's a real relief.
Any word on a Step 2 date for my grievance? And who's representing me at the table?
Step 2 is Tuesday at 10 in the labor relations conference room. I'm representing you with Carla Simmons from the committee sitting in.
Coolant is pooling under the conveyor at station 14 on line 3 — somebody's going to slip. Please get it inspected.
Inspected and red-tagged — the leak's been reported to maintenance and the area's roped off until it's cleaned and the seal is fixed. Thanks for reporting it.
I filed for short-term disability three weeks ago after surgery and haven't heard back. Can you check where my claim stands?
I pulled your claim — it's approved and the first payment posts with the next benefit cycle. I'll email you the determination letter so you have it on file.
